title: Review the uncommitted changes. Context: # Coder Handoff ## Task Canon v3.1 スクリプト実装のコミット — canon-launch.sh改修 + coder/verifier/shipper新規 + SKILL.md更新をgit commitする ## Changed Files .gitignore assets/hooks/context-monitor.sh assets/scripts/canon-launch.sh assets/skills/agent-memory/experience.jsonl ass...
summary_text: 1. High: the new shipper will auto-stage every untracked file in the repo, not just Canon outputs. [canon-v3-shipper.sh#L88-L95](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-shipper.sh#L88) adds everything from `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` except `.canon/`. In this worktree that includes `.project-os/`, `.claude/`, many `correction-pairs/`, and other generated artifacts, so a verified Canon run would commit a large unrelated snapshot. 2. High: the retry loop contract is broken, so `retry max 3 -> needs-human` does not actually work. [canon-v3-coder.sh#L22-L25](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-coder.sh#L22) deletes `pending-fix.md` as soon as Coder reads it, [canon-v3-coder.sh#L63-L69](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-coder.sh#L63) does not restore it on implementation failure, and [canon-v3-verifier.sh#L157-L164](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L157) always recreates the file with `retry: 1`. That means retries never increment and a failed Coder run can drop the only queued fix request entirely. 3. High: the protected-branch safety claim is contradicted by the implementation. [canon-v3-shipper.sh#L4](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-shipper.sh#L4) says “never push to main/master directly”, but [canon-v3-shipper.sh#L106-L111](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-shipper.sh#L106) does a plain `git push` on `main`/`master`. This is an automatic publication path on the repo’s primary branch with no human gate. 4. Medium: Verifier hard-depends on `gtimeout` with no fallback. [canon-v3-verifier.sh#L56-L63](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L56) shells out to `gtimeout` for every stage, but the launcher never checks for it and there is no fallback to `timeout` or bare execution. On a normal macOS setup without `coreutils`, verification fails immediately and starts queueing bogus fix work. 5. Medium: review execution failures are being flattened to success status. [canon-v3-verifier.sh#L97-L109](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L97) uses `out=$(...) || true` and then reads `PIPESTATUS`, which is unset there; in practice `rc` becomes `0` even when `codex exec` exits nonzero. That means real review infrastructure failures are misclassified as low-score reviews instead of “review execution error”. `bash -n` passes for the touched shell scripts, but I did not run an end-to-end `cmux`/`codex`/`gh` workflow. SCORE: 4/10
